title: When I Survey the Wondrous Cross

L: 1/4

metadata.composer: 'Duke Street' John Hatton, 1793 "Finest of the Wheat No. 3" Hymnal, 1904
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2014 Revision.
c_lines: Music: 'Duke Street' John Hatton, 1793.  Setting: "Finest of the Wheat No. 3" Hymnal, 1904.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2014 Revision.

1.raw_text: When I survey the wondrous cross On which the Prince of Glory died, My richest gain I count but loss, And pour contempt on all my pride.

2.raw_text: Forbid it, Lord, that I should boast, Save in the death of Christ my God! All the vain things that charm me most, I sacrifice them to His blood.

3.raw_text: See from His head, His hands, His feet, Sorrow and love flow mingled down! Did e'er such love and sorrow meet, Or thorns compose so rich a crown?

4.raw_text: His dying crimson, like a robe, Spreads o'er His body on the tree; Then I am dead to all the globe, And all the globe is dead to me.

5.raw_text: Were the whole realm of nature mine, That were a present far too small; Love so amazing, so divine, Demands my soul, my life, my all.
